name: Spaghetti with Garlic and Olive Oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a small skillet over medium heat. Add the thinly sliced garlic and cook until it turns opaque.
2. Add the red chile flakes and salt to the pan. Squeeze the juice of half a lemon into the hot oil.
3. Turn off the heat. Allow the garlic to turn light golden at the edges. The garlic will continue to cook in the hot oil.
4. When the oil has cooled somewhat, add the chopped parsley and stir to combine.
5. Meanwhile, bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the spaghetti until al dente, then drain well.
6. Add the cooked spaghetti to the pan with the garlic oil mixture and toss to coat.
7. If desired, drizzle additional extra-virgin olive oil over the pasta.
8. Toss in a handful of garlicky breadcrumbs (made by combining toasted garlic with bread crumbs, salt, and pepper) for added flavor.
The recipe was featured by Chefs Mary Sue Milliken and Susan Feniger on their weekly radio program Good Food on KCRW 89.9FM in Los Angeles. It includes recipes from their restaurant (the Border Grill), their cookbooks (Mesa Mexicana and City Cuisine), or others' cookbooks.
